zh.lifeSummary: 當William Deincourt, Second Baron d'Eynecourt於大約1301年出生在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾布藍可尼時，他的父親John Deincourt30歲，母親Agnes de Percy35歲。他於1326年3月26日之前與Millicent la Zouche在英國林可爾史爾布藍可尼結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1364年6月2日在他的家鄉享年64歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾布藍可尼。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Deincourt, Second Baron d'Eynecourt于大约1301年出生在英国英格兰林可爾史爾布藍可尼时，他的父亲John Deincourt30岁，母亲Agnes de Percy35岁。他于1326年3月26日之前与Millicent la Zouche在英国林可爾史爾布藍可尼结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1364年6月2日在他的家乡享年64岁，埋葬在英国英格兰林可爾史爾布藍可尼。

nameMeanings.name: Second
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French Sicard , reflecting the characteristic Canadian and American French rounding of the -ard ending. Compare Secor and Sicord .
History: The Secords are descendants of Ambroise Sicard, a Huguenot refugee from France (see   Sicard  ).

variants.name: Sicard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Adrien, Albertine, Aude, Benoit, Camille, Elzear, Francois, Jacques, Jean-Paul, Marcel, Monique, Numa.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Sighard, composed of the elements sig ‘victory’ and hard ‘hard, strong’. Compare Seacord , Secor , Secord , Secore , Secoy , and Sicord .

History: Jean Sicard from Mornac-sur-Seudre in Charente-Maritime, France, married Catherine Lauzon in Montreal, QC, in 1681. Jean-Baptiste Sicard, sieur de Carufel (see Carufel ), from Castres in Tarn, France, married Geneviève Raté in Saint-Pierre, Île d'Orléans, QC, in 1694. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its altered forms Sicord and Seacord) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. The Huguenot ancestor was Ambroise Sicard from France, who arrived with his wife and children in NY in the 1680s via England. His descendants (also) bear the surnames Seacord, Secor, Secord, and Secoy.

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Deincourt, Second Baron d'Eynecourt was born about 1301, in Blankney, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Deincourt, was 30 and his mother, Agnes de Percy, was 35. He married Millicent la Zouche before 26 March 1326, in Blankney, Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 2 June 1364, in his hometown, at the age of 64, and was buried in Blankney, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Deincourt, Second Baron d'Eynecourt est né environ 1301, à Blankney, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Deincourt, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Percy, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Millicent la Zouche avant 26 mars 1326, à Blankney, Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 2 juin 1364, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterré à Blankney, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Deincourt, Second Baron d'Eynecourt um 1301 in Blankney, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Deincourt 30 und seine Mutter Agnes de Percy 35. Er hat vor 26. März 1326 in Blankney, Lincolnshire, England Millicent la Zouche geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 2. Juni 1364 im Alter von 64 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Blankney, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Deincourt, Second Baron d'Eynecourt è nato il circa 1301 nel Blankney, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John Deincourt, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Agnes de Percy, aveva 35 anni. Lui sposato Millicent la Zouche prima del 26 marzo 1326, nel Blankney, Lincolnshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 2 giugno 1364, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolto a Blankney, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: William Deincourt, Second Baron d'Eynecourtが約1301年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアブランクニーで生まれた時，父John Deincourtは30歳，母Agnes de Percyは35歳であった。1326年3月26日以前にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアブランクニーでMillicent la Zoucheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1364年6月2日に故郷で64歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアブランクニーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Deincourt, Second Baron d'Eynecourt(이)가 대략 1301년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 블랭니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Deincourt(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes de Percy(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1326년 3월 26일 이전 영국 링코실르 블랭니에서 Millicent la Zouche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1364년 6월 2일 그의 고향에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 블랭니에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Deincourt, Second Baron d'Eynecourt nasceu aproximadamente 1301, em Blankney, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Deincourt, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de Percy, tinha 35 anos. Ele casou-se com Millicent la Zouche antes 26 de março de 1326, em Blankney, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 2 de junho de 1364, em sua cidade natal, com 64 anos, e foi sepultado em Blankney, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Deincourt, Second Baron d'Eynecourt родился около 1301 г. в местности бланни, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Deincourt было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Percy, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Millicent la Zouche перед 26 марта 1326 г. в местности бланни, линколншир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 2 июня 1364 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 64 лет и был похоронен в местности бланни, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Deincourt, Second Baron d'Eynecourt nació aproximadamente 1301, en Blankney, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Deincourt, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Agnes de Percy, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Millicent la Zouche antes de 26 de marzo de 1326, en Blankney, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 2 de junio de 1364, en su pueblo, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultado en Blankney, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
